config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, martabat, atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun ketika hak tiap orang dilindungi dengan adil. Kalau prioritas selalu diberikan pada masyarakat tanpa batas yang jelas, penyalahgunaan kekuasaan jadi lebih mudah terjadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should have priority in some situations, but never as a blanket rule over individual rights. From my perspective in China, I have seen how collective goals can create order, stability, and faster progress, and I do not dismiss that value. But I also do not accept the idea that “society” can be used to silence dissent, weaken due process, or sacrifice minorities whenever it is convenient. A healthy society is not one where the individual is always first, but one where individual rights are protected precisely so that the collective power does not become arbitrary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, sementara hak orang kecil, minoritas, atau yang tidak punya kuasa justru paling mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas; tanpa itu, “masyarakat” bisa berubah jadi dalih bagi penyalahgunaan wewenang. Yang sehat menurut saya bukan mendahulukan salah satu secara mutlak, melainkan memastikan kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa merendahkan martabat dan kebebasan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general principle, because in my view society is strongest when individual rights are treated as a hard boundary, not as a convenience to be overridden. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people value social order, collective goals, and stability, especially when disorder can hurt ordinary families, but I also know how easily “the public interest” can become a vague excuse for excessive control. The point is not to put the individual above society in every case, but to make sure society never gets unlimited power to define its own interests. If rights can be sacrificed whenever officials claim it is necessary, then the result is not a healthy society but a fragile one built on fear and silence.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tindakan yang merugikan warga biasa, terutama minoritas dan orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas; kalau tidak, masyarakat justru bisa menjadi alat untuk menekan kebebasan dan martabat individu. Saya juga tidak setuju bahwa stabilitas otomatis lebih utama, karena stabilitas yang dibangun di atas ketakutan dan pembungkaman bukanlah kebaikan bersama yang sehat. Yang masuk akal menurut saya adalah menyeimbangkan kepentingan publik dengan perlindungan hak individu, bukan memberi prioritas mutlak pada salah satunya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general rule, because in my view society should not be given automatic priority over the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability, coordination, and collective goals, but I also know how quickly “the interests of society” can be used as a broad excuse to ignore due process, silence criticism, or sacrifice weaker groups. A society that treats individual rights as negotiable whenever it is convenient may look orderly on the surface, but it becomes brittle and unfair underneath. For me, the right balance is clear: society matters, but individual rights must remain the boundary that society cannot cross.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
